23.reason: [Loop condition should be i < n or i <= n-1 because array indices go from 0 to n-1; printing a[n] accesses out-of-bounds memory]

28.reason: [Loop condition should be j <= n only if n is the last valid index. But since 'n' passed is n-1 from main, this should be j <= n (which is correct), BUT the base case uses 'begin == n', so the loop should run from begin to n inclusive. However, the real issue is in the print loop above.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Loop condition should be j <= n only if n is the last valid index. But since 'n' passed is n-1 from main, this should be j <= n (which is correct), BUT the base case uses 'begin == n', so the loop should run from begin to n inclusive. However, the real issue is in the print loop above.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [Loop condition should be j <= n only if n is the last valid index. However, in main(), perm is called with n-1 as the third argument, so 'n' here actually represents the last index. But the loop should run while j <= n (which is correct given the call), however the base case uses begin == n which is correct. The real issue is in the print loop above.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.5 , reason:  [Loop condition should be j < n, not j <= n. This leads to accessing a[n], which is out of bounds for an array of size n (indices 0 to n-1).]
